source.title: Accident Investigation Report - Noble Stanley Lafosse Drillship

event.top_event: Loss of Dynamic Positioning and Unplanned Emergency Disconnect Sequence Activation
actions_taken: Emergency Disconnect Sequence (EDS) manually activated by rig floor personnel
Lower Marine Riser Package (LMRP) sheared and detached
Barrier covers installed over emergency fuel shut off valves post-incident
Task Risk Assessment (TRA) process implemented retroactively
event.summary: On 28 MAR 2025 at 1643 hours, an Able Body Seaman 1 (AB1) conducting routine accommodation fire dampener testing alone mistakenly operated the wrong switch panel in the Forward Emergency Gear Locker, inadvertently actuating emergency fuel shut off valves. This caused sequential diesel generator failures, loss of dynamic positioning capability, and drillship drift off station. The rig floor manually activated EDS, shearing the drill pipe and detaching the LMRP, resulting in discharge of 2436 barrels of brine into the Gulf of America and $2.6M in equipment damage.
recommendations: Implement mandatory Task Risk Assessment (TRA) prior to all maintenance tasks
Establish physical barriers and warning signs on emergency fuel shut off valves
Enforce Stop Work Authority protocols when personnel express uncertainty
Require paired work assignments for critical maintenance tasks
Improve panel labeling and spatial separation of critical control systems

hazards.name: Pressurized fuel system in engine room
Dynamic positioning system dependency for station keeping

threats.name: Inadvertent actuation of emergency fuel shut off valves

consequences.name: Loss of containment - brine discharge to sea
Structural damage to subsea equipment (LMRP, BOP stack)

controls.name: Task Risk Assessment (TRA) prior to maintenance work
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify hazards and clarify work procedures

supporting_text: The Task Risk Assessment (TRA), required prior to work, was not performed until after the incident occurred
NSL personnel did not perform a TRA prior to commencement of job task in accordance with Noble company policy
Through the task risk assessment, personnel would have been afforded the opportunity to address potential hazards, personnel awareness of plan, the correct execution of work assignment

controls.name: Stop Work Authority execution by personnel
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control allowing workers to halt unsafe work
controls.lod_basis: Company policy empowering personnel to stop work when uncertain

supporting_text: Failure to execute 'Stop Work Authority' when AB1 felt uncertainty regarding completing the tasks solo
AB1 conducted unsafe operations by not verifying dampener line-item numbers or using stop work authority when he felt unsure of required work task
NSL management believes AB1 felt self-perceived pressure and rushed through the task

controls.name: Paired work assignment for critical maintenance tasks
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control requiring two personnel for critical work
controls.lod_basis: Standard practice for critical equipment maintenance

supporting_text: the Noble Drilling Company Able Body seaman 1 (AB1), who typically would be paired with another colleague, was tasked with testing the accommodation fire dampeners alone
No assistance from the NSL Bosun, also described as Marine Department Supervisor on duty and clear communications were not provided to AB1

controls.name: Physical barriers and warning signs on emergency fuel shut off valves
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent inadvertent valve actuation

supporting_text: the emergency fuel shut off valves were not protected with barriers or warning signs to prevent inadvertent functioning by ship personnel
rig management had installed barrier covers over the emergency fuel shut off valves, to mitigate future occurrences of personnel inadvertently operating quick close of the fuel valves

controls.name: Panel labeling and spatial separation of control systems
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent confusion between similar control panels
controls.lod_basis: Design separation to prevent human error

supporting_text: BSEE inspectors observed both the accommodation dampeners and emergency fuel shut off valves were within 10-12 feet of one another
Observation of the valves showed clear and legible labeling at each panel for the designated equipment
mistakenly operating the wrong switch panel

controls.name: Dynamic Positioning Yellow and Red Alert system
controls.barrier_role: Detection and alarm system for DP operational concerns
controls.lod_basis: Real-time monitoring and alerting of DP system status

supporting_text: A Dynamic Positioning (DP) Yellow Alert was activated, indicating initial operational concerns
A DP Red Alert activated, with the rig 7.5 meters from the setpoint. The senior DPO alerts rig floor of Red Zone Panel alert

controls.name: Emergency Disconnect Sequence (EDS) manual activation
controls.barrier_role: Emergency response to disconnect wellhead when DP is lost
controls.lod_basis: Mechanical disconnect system to prevent riser damage

supporting_text: the rig floor manually activated the Emergency Disconnect Sequence (EDS) which sheared the 5.875 in., Z140, 34.21lb/ft drill pipe and successfully detached the Lower Marine Riser Package (LMRP)
the EDS was activated by rig floor personnel, unlatching 58 seconds later at 25.6 meters from the setpoint

controls.name: Diesel generator redundancy and thruster system
controls.barrier_role: Backup power and propulsion for station keeping recovery
controls.lod_basis: Multiple independent power sources and thrusters

supporting_text: DG #1 (port engine room) restored online
DG #6 (starboard engine room) restored online
All six thrusters and three diesel generators fully operational, restoring DP control

people.competence_value: adequate but insufficient for solo work

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields are evidence-based. null used for unknown values.
